</explanation>

raw_response_text:
<explanation>
The target image shows a large, single flower with broad, rounded petals and a central area that is slightly lighter in color. This structure is visually similar to the flowers in class 88 (nasturtium), which feature large, overlapping petals and a distinct central throat. While the color is bright orange/red rather than the red/yellow seen in the examples, the petal shape and overall flower morphology match class 88 more closely than the spiky texture of class 34, the large petal structure of class 81 (clematis), or the delicate, multi-petal structure of class 71 (azalea).
</explanation>

<response>88</response>
